# <!-- generated by tools/generate-rules.py -->
# Source: standards/universal/CI.md
# Regenerate with: python tools/generate-rules.py --file CI.md
paths:
  - "**/.github/workflows/*.yml"
  - "**/.github/workflows/*.yaml"
  - "**/.releaserc"
  - "**/.releaserc.*"
  - "**/release.config.*"
  - "**/VERSION"
  - "**/CHANGELOG.md"

  - "**/pyproject.toml"
  - "**/Cargo.toml"
  - "**/.hyperi-ci.yaml"
  - "**/Makefile"
detect_markers:
  - "file:.releaserc"
  - "glob:.releaserc.*"
  - "glob:release.config.*"
  - "file:VERSION"
  - "dir:.github"
  - "file:.hyperi-ci.yaml"
  - "dir:ci"
source: universal/CI.md
---

# hyperi-ci (Polyglot CI/CD Tool)

**Detection:** `.hyperi-ci.yaml` in project root ‚Üí all CI operations go through `hyperi-ci`.

> ‚öÝÔ∏è hyperi-ci is under active development. Check [repo docs](https://github.com/hyperi-io/hyperi-ci) and `CHANGELOG.md` for latest.

## Install

```bash
uv tool install hyperi-ci
# fallback: pip install --user hyperi-ci
```

## CLI Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `hyperi-ci check` | **Pre-push validation (quality + test) ‚Äî run before every push** |
| `hyperi-ci check --quick` | Quality only (skip tests) |
| `hyperi-ci check --full` | Quality + test + build |
| `hyperi-ci run quality` | Lint, format, type check, security audit |
| `hyperi-ci run test` | Tests with coverage |
| `hyperi-ci run build` | Build artifacts |
| `hyperi-ci run publish` | **CI only ‚Äî never run locally** |
| `hyperi-ci detect` | Show detected language |
| `hyperi-ci config` | Show merged config as JSON |
| `hyperi-ci trigger [--watch]` | Trigger GitHub Actions workflow |
| `hyperi-ci watch` | Watch latest CI run |
| `hyperi-ci logs [--failed]` | Show run logs (optionally failed only) |
| `hyperi-ci init` | Scaffold project (`.hyperi-ci.yaml`, `Makefile`, workflow, `.releaserc.yaml`) |

## Use hyperi-ci, Not Direct Tools

| ‚ùå Don't | ‚úÖ Do | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `ruff check .` | `hyperi-ci run quality` / `make quality` | hyperi-ci handles config, flags, tool selection |
| `pytest` | `hyperi-ci run test` / `make test` | |
| `cargo build` | `hyperi-ci run build` / `make build` | |
| Manual linter calls | `hyperi-ci run quality` | |

## Makefile Targets

```bash
make check      # Pre-push validation (quality + test) ‚Äî USE THIS
make quality    # All quality checks
make test       # Tests
make build      # Build artifacts
make ci         # quality + test + build (full local CI)
```

## MANDATORY: Pre-Push Check

**NEVER push without running `hyperi-ci check` first.** If user asks to push, run check first. Only skip if user explicitly says so.

**Workflow:**
1. Write code on topic branch or `main` ‚Äî **NEVER on `release`**
2. `hyperi-ci check`
3. Fix failures, amend or new commit
4. `git pull --rebase origin main` (sync semantic-release commits)
5. `git push origin main` (or merge topic branch)
6. Create PR: `main ‚Üí release` (**NEVER push directly to release**)

## Semantic Release

**Detection:** `.releaserc`, `release.config.*`, `VERSION` file, or workflow referencing `semantic-release`.

### NEVER Do (semantic-release owns these)

- ‚ùå Edit `VERSION` ‚Äî written by `@semantic-release/exec`
- ‚ùå Edit `CHANGELOG.md` ‚Äî generated by `@semantic-release/changelog`
- ‚ùå Bump version in `package.json` / `pyproject.toml` / `Cargo.toml`
- ‚ùå Create git tags or GitHub releases
- ‚ùå Suggest version bumps in PRs

### Local Branch 1 Commit Behind ‚Äî This Is Normal

After push, semantic-release creates `chore(release): X.Y.Z [skip ci]` and pushes it back. **Before every push:** `git pull --rebase origin main`. Never force-push over it.

### Commit Messages Drive Releases

| Prefix | Effect |
|---|---|
| `fix:` | PATCH bump (use most of the time) |
| `feat:` | MINOR bump (genuinely new user-facing features only) |
| `BREAKING CHANGE:` in body | MAJOR bump ‚Äî **NEVER write automatically; require explicit user confirmation** |
| `chore:` / `docs:` / `ci:` / `test:` | No release |

## CI Workflows

- Prefer reusable workflows / composite actions over copy-paste
- Pin actions to full SHA, not tags: `uses: actions/checkout@<sha>`
- `vars.*` for non-secret config, `secrets.*` for credentials
- Default runner: `${{ vars.GH_RUNNER_DEFAULT || 'ubuntu-latest' }}`

## Legacy CI Submodule

**Detection:** `ci/` git submodule (typically `hyperi-io/ci`) present **without** `.hyperi-ci.yaml`.

- ‚ùå Do NOT use scripts from `ci/` submodule ‚Äî deprecated, may not work
- ‚úÖ Tell user: "This project uses the legacy ci/ submodule. Run `hyperi-ci migrate` to upgrade."
- `hyperi-ci migrate` generates `.hyperi-ci.yaml`, `Makefile`, workflow; old `ci/` submodule can then be removed
